pages.raw_text:
1t Is reported today and not
denied that the local brotherhoods
of engineers and trainmen have ex-
PEELED all the employees Of the
Graves system who were members
of their organization and who ac-
cepted the 10 per cent cut in wages
made recently by the Inland En-
pire Co. Members of the brother
hoods refuse to discuss the matter
one way or the other. but they adj
NIT they have heard that some such
action was taken.


1t was stated today by employees
of the Traction lines that the men,
bers of the brotherhoods employed
on the Inland Empire interurban
system were making application to
the local street Garments union as
a result of their being ousted from
the big railroad organizations


The exact motive for the action
Of the railway men in compelling
the men employed on the Inland
Empire system to give up their
membership will probably never be
known. Engineers receive about
so cents per hour and overtime for
certain conditions. and 1t Is not
known that they objected to mem
bers securing employment on cleo,
trio lines at 80 cents per hour,
which was the wage paid by the In.
land Empire Co. until the recent
cut of 10 per cent was made


All motor men on the interurban
lines have to hold engineers II.
censes. Practically all the men en-
ployed in this line of business have
at one time or another been en-
ployed on steam lines and are mem.
bers of the Brotherhood Of En-
gineers.


1t is surmised that the brother
hoods could not stand for their
members accepting a reduced wage
scale without authority.
